# pmcontrols

> Project scheduling and earned value control for Python: critical path
> (CPM), PERT with Monte Carlo schedule risk, minimum-cost crashing as a
> linear program, and EVM with Lipke's earned schedule - every released
> number reproduced against published reference values on every CI run.

pmcontrols exists because project-control arithmetic (backward passes,
earned-value indices, earned schedule) is easy to state and easy to get
subtly wrong, and almost all of it currently happens in unaudited Excel.
Each analysis returns one consistent `Result` object: `.stats` (named
scalars), `.table` (tidy pandas DataFrame), `.alerts`, `.ok` (a pass/fail
gate), `.summary()` (plain-language verdict) and `.to_dict()` (a JSON-safe,
versioned payload with provenance). Inputs are plain dict records, so an
agent can build them straight from a table or a user's description.

Install with `pip install pmcontrols` and import as `import pmcontrols as pm`.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/): what the library does and the sixty-second tour.
- [API and grammar](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/reference/api/): the frozen `Result`/`PMB` contract and every public function signature.
- [Validation](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/reference/validation/): how results are checked against published reference values.

## Guides

- [Critical path in Python](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/guides/critical-path/): `pm.cpm` - forward/backward pass, slack, the critical path.
- [PERT and schedule risk](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/guides/pert-risk/): `pm.pert` - three-point estimates, Monte Carlo completion distribution, per-activity criticality index.
- [Schedule crashing as a linear program](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/guides/crashing/): `pm.crash` - the cheapest compression meeting a deadline, solved optimally.
- [Earned value and earned schedule](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/guides/earned-value/): `pm.plan`/`pm.evm` - the full indicator set plus Lipke's time-based ES, SPI(t) and IEAC(t).
- [EVM control as a cron job](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/guides/evm-cron/): `r.ok` as an automation primitive for headless status gates.

## For agents

- [Project control is not a language task](https://arikanatakan.github.io/pmcontrols/agents/): why an agent should call a validated function instead of doing the schedule and earned-value math in tokens.
